summary: Do not ship the design as settled: its “full test migration” omits existing direct callers that will fail when `now` becomes required and the globals are deleted.
triage: review-gate SKILL.md rules govern: render EVERY finding as a full table row, the human decides each row (accept/reject/defer/question — free text), echo back before applying, max 2 rounds, append decisions.md and COMMIT it with this artifact.

findings.title: Required-parameter migration omits two dependent test suites
findings.body: The design limits migration to `test_stale_reading.py` and `test_codex_reading.py`, but the repository has seven direct `codex_poll()` calls in `tests/test_codex_credentials.py`; that suite also derives its JWT-expiry stub from `mod.NOW_EPOCH`, which the design deletes. `tests/test_entry_point.py` additionally replaces both poll functions with zero-argument lambdas, while the redesigned `main()` will call them with `now`. Building the system as designed therefore causes deterministic test failures and leaves a credential-rotation suite without its clock fixture. This contradicts the claim that there is no unserved caller and that the migration is full.
findings.recommendation: Expand the declared migration scope to inventory and update every direct poll caller. Pass an explicit reference time in `test_codex_credentials.py`, derive `jwt_exp` from that value, and change the entry-point stubs to accept and optionally assert the supplied `now` argument.

next_steps: Revise the design’s target and full-migration sections to include `tests/test_codex_credentials.py` and `tests/test_entry_point.py`.
Re-run a repository-wide caller search before marking the design settled.
